locator: Vegetius, De Re Militari, Book I (c. 390 AD); Clarke translation (1767); text verified at https://archive.org/stream/pdfy-sOkC3FmoLlr4C6zz/The+Military+Institutions+Of+The+Romans+[De+Re+Militari]_djvu.txt
text: The security established by long peace has altered their dispositions, drawn them off from military to civil pursuits and infused into them a love of idleness and ease.

locator: Augustine of Hippo, City of God, Book V, Chapter 15 (c. 413–426 AD); Marcus Dods translation; text verified at https://www.newadvent.org/fathers/120105.htm
text: They despised their own private affairs for the sake of the republic, and for its treasury resisted avarice, consulted for the good of their country with a spirit of freedom.
